behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: classifier -> request builder/client. A unit test of prompt-building logic could miss wiring bugs where the sent request omits labels. Real OS/network is not required; behavior text is about request contents, not live transport/model quality, so System/Agentic are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a fake in-process API client that records outbound payload. Invoke classify() with a taxonomy and assert the captured request contains the full label list (no omissions/reordering loss per contract).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The failure can occur at response-handoff level (API adapter output not passed through taxonomy guard). Pure unit normalization tests may pass while integration path bypasses normalization. No real infrastructure or real model judgment is needed; behavior text focuses on guarding returned labels.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process fake API client returning an out-of-taxonomy label. Call public classify() and assert result is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly one-step normalization determinism for identical input; it is local function/object logic with no required module/IO boundary. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text "normalization step" and "identical raw responses".
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function/object repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires "live API calls" and label consistency tied to provider/model outputs. A mock would hide real model/provider variance, so Integration is too low. It is not a multi-step agent pipeline; repeated single-step calls do not require Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls against the provider for fixed input and taxonomy, logging provider raw outputs and final labels. Assert label remains stable across runs where provider response is unchanged; allow divergence only when provider response differs.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is policy enforcement + dispatch handoff (ensuring over-budget input is truncated/rejected before send). Unit tests of tokenizer/policy alone can miss bypass in the classify path. Behavior text is pre-dispatch construction, so no real network/model output is required; System/Agentic are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Configure small budget, use fake API client spy, and call classify() with oversized document. Assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) and verify outbound request respects budget or no dispatch occurs when reject policy is configured.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real AI provider call and real provider-reported usage; stubs can always report compliant usage and miss true provider metering/tokenization behavior. Higher than System is justified because behavior execution path depends on real AI API call semantics. Workflow is disqualified: only one model call step is required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classify() calls for representative documents with configured budget, capture provider-reported token usage metadata, and assert each call's reported usage <= configured per-call budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration between API client wrapper and classifier control flow. In-process fake returning rate-limit then success can reproduce it; real OS/network is not required, so System is rejected. Correctness does not depend on model output quality, so Agentic/Workflow are rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake API client scripted to return rate-limit error (e.g., 429/typed signal) then success. Assert classify() waits per retry policy (or uses injected clock/backoff hook) and eventually returns label instead of propagating immediate error.
